news.title: NCLT approves Mantra Properties’ resolution plan for stalled project in Worli
news.para: The NCLT Mumbai Bench has approved Pune-based Mantra Properties & Developers’ resolution plan to revive a stalled Worli residential project in Mumbai with an estimated revenue potential of around Rs 2,000 crore.

news.title: Centre approves over 6.18 lakh PMAY-Gramin houses for Uttar Pradesh
news.para: Farmers in Uttar Pradesh will benefit from an extended MSP procurement period for wheat, gram, and lentils until July 8. Additionally, over 6.18 lakh rural houses have been sanctioned under the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana-Gramin's new phase (2024-29). These decisions, aimed at supporting farmers and boosting rural housing, were confirmed by Union Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan during a review with Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath.

news.title: Consumer court orders Mumbai-based builder to refund money to buyer for not delivering flat
news.para: A Mumbai consumer commission has ordered two builders to refund Rs 1.05 crore with interest to a couple who lost their life savings. The builders failed to deliver a flat after accepting full payment and then sold it to another person. The couple endured over a decade of distress, facing bounced cheques and unfulfilled promises.

news.title: Karnataka government to simplify e-Swathu norms for gramathana properties
news.para: Rural property owners in Karnataka are set to receive a significant boost as the government plans to simplify rules for e-Swathu records.  This initiative aims to provide ownership documents for approximately 65 lakh inherited properties within gramathana limits lacking formal titles.  The process, expected to be completed within 100 days, prioritizes genuine occupants while safeguarding government lands.  An extension for Form 11B applications is also announced.

news.title: Karnataka offers one-time waiver on OCs for certain residential buildings completed by May 2026
news.para: Karnataka government has provided a one-time waiver on occupancy certificates for specific residential buildings completed by May 31, 2026. This applies to G+3 or stilt plus four-storey homes up to 2,400 sq ft in Hescom-jurisdiction districts. Eligible buildings that applied for permanent electricity, had temporary connections, or were built without any connection can now secure permanent power. The exemption also covers various agricultural structures.

news.title: Land sharks selling government plots in Noida villages, authority issues public notice
news.para: Noida Authority is cracking down on illegal constructions and land encroachments in Salarpur, Bhangel, and Hajipur villages.  Officials are demolishing and sealing unauthorized structures on notified and acquired plots, warning residents against investing in such properties.  The Authority urges the public to verify land details before any transactions to prevent financial losses due to illegal occupation.

news.title: Built-to-suit 2.0: The evolution of customized workspaces in India
news.para: India's commercial real estate is witnessing a transformation with Built-to-Suit 2.0, integrating Grade A spaces, human-centric design, tech, and ESG wellness. This new model is a strategic partnership, moving beyond basic customization to create adaptive environments reflecting brand identity and supporting diverse work modes. Accelerated by hybrid work and city expansion, it prioritizes employee experience and productivity, making the workplace a critical business driver.

news.title: Lucknow development body forms new rule for building plan nod over fire safety
news.para: Following a devastating fire, Lucknow Development Authority now mandates notarized fire safety affidavits for all building plan approvals. Previously, smaller buildings were exempt, but new rules require owners to certify compliance. This move aims to enhance city-wide fire safety, with inspections and strict action planned for violations. Only single-story residential units under four meters are exempt.

news.title: ED raids in Ghaziabad, Delhi in alleged housing society fraud case
news.para: The search operation is underway and being conducted by ED's Lucknow zonal office under Section 17 of the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA) in connection with the probe against Sandeep Singh, promoter of Shrasth Propbuild Pvt Ltd, and office-bearers of Seva Suraksha Sahkari Awas Samiti.

news.title: Chhattisgarh CM orders statewide safety audit of buildings
news.para: Following recent fire tragedies nationwide, Chhattisgarh Chief Minister Vishnu Deo Sai has mandated a thorough safety audit of all multi-storey buildings, including residential complexes, commercial spaces, and educational institutions. This directive emphasizes strict adherence to fire safety norms, availability of equipment, and emergency preparedness. Authorities are tasked with inspecting buildings for compliance and taking stringent action against violations to ensure public safety.

news.title: Britain's Berkeley Group calls for tax and regulatory rethink after 15% profit fall
news.para: British homebuilder Berkeley Group is urging the government to slash taxes and regulations, citing a 15% profit drop and London's severe housing shortage.  The company believes easing burdens and cutting stamp duty are crucial to attract investment and boost construction.  Despite economic headwinds, Berkeley maintains its profit outlook, but analysts question political willingness to heed industry advice amidst leadership changes.

news.title: India office leasing rises 6% to 35.7 million sq ft in H1 2026: Report
news.para: According to Colliers India, Grade-A office leasing across the top seven cities stood at 17.4 million sq ft in Q2 2026, down 2% year-on-year. Bengaluru and Hyderabad together accounted for nearly half of office demand in the first half of 2026.

news.title: Lucknow: LDA seals 71 centres, slaps notices on 83 building owners over fire safety norms
news.para: Following a tragic fire that claimed 15 lives, Lucknow authorities have cracked down on building safety violations.  Seventy-one establishments were sealed and 83 owners received notices for flouting fire safety and building norms.  This city-wide enforcement, targeting various commercial entities, aims to prevent future disasters and will continue for three weeks, with further action promised for non-compliance.

news.title: Rajasthan: Government pattas undercut crackdown on illegal colonies
news.para: Rajasthan's government faces criticism for regularizing unauthorized colonies via Urban Service Camps, a move town planners and officials argue incentivizes violations. By relaxing rules like site inspections for property regularization, the policy is seen as rewarding illegal construction. Critics warn this practice encourages builders to develop unauthorized settlements, expecting future government leniency, and could compromise safety due to inadequate access in these areas.

news.title: Goa: GIDC allots three plots for ambitious IT park project
news.para: Goa's Verna industrial estate is set to host the 'Hello World Innovation & Research Park,' with three of its six large plots already allocated to a Mumbai-based developer. This ambitious project, expected to create 20,000-30,000 jobs, aims to attract skilled professionals back to the state.  The developer has sought clarity on infrastructure timelines, raising concerns about potential delays impacting project commencement.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh government to set up 500 GCC units by 2031: CM
news.para: Uttar Pradesh is positioning itself as India's next major hub for Global Capability Centres and technology investments. Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath invited global firms to "build the future in Uttar Pradesh," highlighting a skilled workforce, improved infrastructure, and investor-friendly policies. The state aims for 40 million sq ft of office space and 500 GCC units by 2031, securing over Rs 50,000 crore in investment commitments during a recent dialogue.

news.title: Tenants told to shift from illegal PGs and guesthouses by June 30 in Gurugram
news.para: Gurugram residents in illegal paying guest accommodations, guesthouses, and commercial establishments operating from residential plots in DLF Phases 1-5 have been urged to find new housing by June 30. The Town and Country Planning department is cracking down on violations of zoning and building norms. Many occupants were unaware of prior notices to property owners, prompting this public advisory to prevent inconvenience from sealing actions.

news.title: Hyderabad development body's Medipally auction nets ₹120 crore amid strong demand
news.para: Hyderabad Metropolitan Development Authority's recent land auction in Medipally proved a resounding success, raking in nearly ₹120 crore from 68 plots. Bids significantly surpassed the reserve price, with one plot fetching ₹80,000 per square yard. This strong market response, despite a court stay on another sale, highlights robust confidence in HMDA-developed layouts and growing demand for planned residential spaces across the region.

news.title: Vedanta announces foray into real estate sector
news.para: Vedanta Ltd has launched a new wholly-owned subsidiary, Vedanta Property Platforms Ltd (VPPL), to enter the real estate sector. This strategic move aims to unlock value from surplus land and non-core assets, potentially funding expansion in its core metals and energy businesses. VPPL, incorporated in Mumbai, will act as a dedicated platform for real estate ventures and asset-light initiatives.
